methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.2B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $8.3B - $25.0B.

data.narrative:
World Labs is a Series A AI & software company founded by Fei-Fei Li, Ben Mildenhall, Christoph Lassner, and Justin Johnson, having raised $1.23B+. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 1 public company (Palantir) and 8 private peers, including Reflection AI, Applied Intuition, Together AI, Waymo, and others in the same sector and stage.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $1.2B total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, yielding $2.5B. Peer-median analysis examines three same-sector, same-stage comparables: Reflection AI at $25.0B, Applied Intuition at $15.0B, and Together AI at $8.3B, producing a median valuation of $15.0B with a range of $8.3B to $25.0B.

The triangulation of these two methods supports a valuation range of $2.1B (low) to $17.2B (high), with a median of $8.7B. The substantial spread reflects divergent signals: the stage-progression method anchored to capital raised suggests a more conservative floor, while the peer-median approach captures the premium multiples commanded by Series A AI companies. Key uncertainties include World Labs' differentiated technology claims relative to peers and the sustainability of valuations in the current AI comp set, which may reflect speculative pricing rather than normalized fundamentals.
